resume.rawText:
Christopher Gross

West Linn, OR
chris@dreem.one
 | (503) 310-5433
Authorized to work in the U.S. | Willing to relocate

Designer & Energy Consultant

Transitioning into UX, Product, and Systems-Focused Roles

Professional Summary

Designer and energy consultant who thrives in collaborative, high-trust environments and complex decision-making situations. Generated $850,000+ in solar contracts within six months through consultative, relationship-driven sales. Founded and scaled a product brand to $2.5M in first-year Amazon sales by aligning product development, customer insight, and execution systems. Known for steady negotiation, clear communication, and building repeatable workflows that reduce chaos and improve outcomes.

Work Experience
Energy Consultant

Sunward Power — Portland, OR
March 2024 – Present

Generated $850,000+ in signed commercial and residential solar contracts within six months.

Conducted energy assessments and feasibility studies for churches, nonprofits, and commercial buildings.

Guided clients through incentive programs including Energy Trust of Oregon, federal tax credits, and grants.

Collaborated with engineering and design teams to develop customized renewable energy systems.

Built an internal peak-shaving calculator app to model demand scenarios and support faster, clearer proposals.

Sales Designer

Reborn Home Solutions — Portland, OR
April 2023 – February 2024

Top regional performer for three consecutive months; maintained 40% close rate and $150K+ monthly revenue.

Designed custom kitchen and bath remodels aligned with client goals and budgets.

Managed projects from design through installation, ensuring client satisfaction and on-time delivery.

Founder / Owner

Dreem LLC — West Linn, OR
November 2015 – February 2024

Founded and scaled an e-commerce brand, generating $2.5M in first-year Amazon sales.

Led product development, supplier sourcing, photography, copywriting, and marketing strategy.

Negotiated supplier contracts and secured capital for product launches.

Built internal systems for forecasting, inventory planning, and customer experience management.

Textbook Reseller

Green World Books — Salem, OR
September 2005 – June 2015

Achieved highest statewide sales volume for five consecutive years.

Trained and managed top-performing buyers while maintaining strong institutional relationships.

Home Remodeler

Gracer West Interiors — Salem, OR
August 2003 – August 2005

Supported full residential remodel projects with focus on craftsmanship and client communication.

Projects (Product + App Development)

Peak Shaving Calculator (Sunward Power) — Built an internal tool to model peak demand / load scenarios and support faster decision-making in commercial proposal workflows.

Workflow Apps (In Progress) — Building two additional internal apps with AI-assisted development focused on automating repetitive work and improving team clarity and throughput. (Code available on GitHub.)

Once I can actually access the repos that contain these apps, I’ll replace “internal tool / in progress” with clean, specific bullets: what each app does, who uses it, and the stack.

Education

Associate of Arts (Minor in Arts & Music)
Portland Community College — Portland, OR

Skills

Design & UX: UI Design, UX Research, Wireframing, Branding, Adobe Photoshop & Illustrator, Web Design, Photography, Copywriting
Energy & Consulting: Commercial Solar Sales, Feasibility Studies, Incentive Programs (Energy Trust of Oregon), Proposal Development, Client Consultation
Business & Operations: Product Development, Negotiation, Project Management, Market Research, Salesforce (CRM), Business Development
App Development (practical): GitHub (projects available upon request)

Portfolio

dreem.one — UX, visual design, product work, and solar proposal examples available upon request.

1.question: In your most recent role, what part of the work felt most like you?

2.question: What part of the role drained you fastest?

3.question: What do others come to you for that isn’t necessarily in your job description?

4.question: What type of challenge feels exciting rather than overwhelming?

5.question: Outside of work, what do you spend time practicing, improving, or maintaining? 2–4 bullets.
